trigger_reason: R766 is rank by mechanical-PASS proximity in E31 (post-filter architectural candidate with kw=2; tree-stream max sim=0.40). Per program_v10.md §2.5 (preserved in v11/v12), top-3 rounds receive full step-13 spec.

A_candidate.name: Hopf-bifurcation gating module
A_candidate.description: Insert a new gating module after each attention layer that conditions the residual update on a learnable bifurcation parameter; the gate has a Hopf-like switching behavior around the bifurcation threshold.

B_control.name: Standard sigmoidal gating with the same number of parameters
B_control.description: Same gating module but with sigmoidal instead of Hopf-bifurcation activation
B_control.distinctive_parameter_value: no new module / same architecture without the new pathway

statistical_test.name: paired t-test A vs B + A vs C

distinguishability_pre_check.rationale: The Hopf-bifurcation gating is structurally distinct from sigmoidal gating in its bifurcation behavior at the threshold; however, in the linearized regime far from bifurcation, the gate behaves identically to sigmoidal. Whether the bifurcation regime is reached depends on the training dynamics.
distinguishability_pre_check.v12_observation: Step 05.5 PASS = architectural-topology-change; candidate has a new learnable module with distinct computational signature from baseline. Distinguishability pre-check leans more toward true/uncertain than v11's typical 'uncertain' default.

notes_for_executor: R766's architectural-topology candidate (post-v12-step-05.5-PASS) has structurally-distinct mechanism from baseline B. The new learnable module adds a distinct computation graph edge.
